items.q: Can sucrose move in both directions in the phloem?
items.correct: Yes — translocation goes from any source (e.g. leaves) to any sink (e.g. roots, fruits), so direction varies seasonally and between phloem sieve tubes.
items.misconception: Phloem always carries sucrose downwards from leaves to roots.
items.random_wrong: Phloem carries water upwards and sucrose downwards in the same tube.
items.why: Students conflate phloem with xylem in terms of being unidirectional; they don't realise source-sink relationships can reverse (e.g. early spring, roots are sources).

items.q: What is the role of the guide RNA in CRISPR-Cas9?
items.correct: It directs Cas9 to the specific DNA sequence to cut by base-pairing with the target.
items.misconception: Guide RNA is the new sequence that gets inserted into the genome.
items.random_wrong: Guide RNA is the enzyme that cuts the DNA.
items.why: Students confuse the guide RNA (which targets) with Cas9 (which cuts) and with any donor DNA (which is inserted) — three distinct components.

items.correct: A series of chemical reactions in cells that break down glucose to release energy as ATP.
items.misconception: Respiration means breathing — inhaling oxygen and exhaling carbon dioxide.
items.random_wrong: Respiration is the process by which plants make their own food using sunlight.
items.why: Everyday English equates 'respiration' with breathing; students rarely separate gas exchange from cellular metabolism.
The answer examiners want is: LH. “FSH” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: After ovulation, progesterone is mainly made by:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Corpus luteum. “Follicle” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In a typical 28-day menstrual cycle, on which day does ovulation occur?
items.why: The real figure is 14. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
